title: FINRA Case 25-01580 — Venu Reddy v. LPL Financial LLC, Waddell & Reed
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-01580
Venu Reddy
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Kansas City, Missouri
LPL Financial LLC, Waddell & Reed, and
Macquarie Capital (USA), Inc.
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Associated Person vs. Members
This case was decided by a majority-public panel.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
Claimant Venu Reddy (“Claimant”) appeared pro se.
For Respondents LPL Financial LLC (“LPL”) and Waddell & Reed (“Waddell”), hereinafter,
collectively referred to as “Remaining Respondents”: Blake M. Edwards, Esq., Gordon Rees
Scully Mansukhani, LLP, Kansas City, Missouri.
Respondent Macquarie Capital (USA) Inc. (“Macquarie”) did not appear.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: August 15, 2025.
Amended Statement of Claim filed on or about: September 25, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: August 15, 2025, and September 25, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by Remaining Respondents on or about: November 20, 2025.
Remaining Respondents signed the Submission Agreement: December 8, 2025.
Macquarie did not file a Statement of Answer or sign the Submission Agreement.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01580
Award Page 2 of 5
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, as amended, Claimant alleged that he was retaliated against and
fraudulently terminated from his employment for downgrading stocks and disseminating and
publishing unfavorable research recommendations.
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer, Remaining Respondents denied the
allegations made in the Amended Statement of Claim and asserted various affirmative defenses.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, as amended, Claimant requested $25,965,374.00 in compensatory
damages, including double back pay and interest.
In the Statement of Answer, Remaining Respondents requested a final judgment in their favor
(1) dismissing with prejudice each and every claim asserted by Claimant; (2) declaring
Remaining Respondents to be the prevailing parties on each dismissed claim; (3) denying
Claimant the judgment and relief sought in his Amended Statement of Claim; and (4) granting
Remaining Respondents such other and further legal and equitable relief as may be just and
proper under the circumstances.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
Macquarie did not file a properly executed Submission Agreement but is required to submit to
arbitration pursuant to the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) and is bound by the
determination of the Panel on all issues submitted.
On September 25, 2025, Claimant filed an Amended Statement of Claim removing Macquarie
as a party to this arbitration matter. Accordingly, the Panel made no determination with respect
to Claimant’s claims against Macquarie.
On November 20, 2025, Remaining Respondents filed a Motion to Dismiss pursuant to Rules
12206, 12504, 13206, and 13504 of the Code. On January 27, 2026, Claimant filed a response
opposing the Motion to Dismiss. On February 17, 2026, Remaining Respondents filed a Reply in
support of the Motion to Dismiss. On February 19, 2026, Claimant filed an additional response
to the Motion to Dismiss. On February 28, 2026, Claimant filed a supplemental submission in
response to the Motion to Dismiss. On March 13, 2026, the Panel heard oral arguments on the
Motion to Dismiss. Herein, the Panel grants the Motion to Dismiss on the following grounds:
The Motion to Dismiss is granted because under FINRA rule 13206 none of Claimant's
claims are eligible for arbitration because more than six years have elapsed from the
occurrence(s) or event(s) giving rise to Claimant's claims. The events giving rise to
Claimant's claims occurred back in 2002 and stem, inter alia, from wrongful discharge.
Moreover, Claimant misunderstands the role that arbitration plays. Claimant is asking the

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01580
Award Page 3 of 5
Panel/FINRA to "prosecute my claims against Macquarie Capital (USA), Inc. on behalf of
the SEC... "
Arbitrators are not employees of FINRA. Arbitrators are neutrals who adjudicate disputes
that occurred within 6 years of the date of filing of an eligible claim. Neither FINRA and
certainly not arbitrators, prosecute claims on behalf of Claimants in this arbitration forum.
Remaining Respondents’ Motion to Dismiss is granted by the Panel without prejudice to any
right Claimant has to file in court; Claimant is not prohibited from pursuing their claims in court
pursuant to Rule 13206(b) of the Code.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, Remaining Respondents’ Motion to Dismiss and all responses
thereto, and the arguments presented at the prehearing conference on March 13, 2026, the
Panel has decided in full and final resolution of the issues submitted for determination as
follows:
1. Claimant’s claims are dismissed in their entirety.
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 2,875.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services administratively waived Claimant’s filing fee of $2,875.00.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as parties, LPL, Waddell, and Macquarie are each assessed the
following:
Remaining Respondents:
Member Surcharge =$ 6,490.00
Member Process Fee =$ 10,950.00

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01580
Award Page 4 of 5
Macquarie:
Member Surcharge =$ 6,490.00
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
Three (3) pre-hearing sessions with the Panel @ $2,370.00/session =$ 7,110.00
Pre-Hearing Conferences: January 20, 2026 1 session
February 23, 2026 1 session
March 13, 2026 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 7,110.00
The Panel has assessed $2,370.00 of the hearing session fees to Claimant.
The Panel has assessed $4,740.00 of the hearing session fees jointly and severally to
Remaining Respondents.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01580
Award Page 5 of 5
ARBITRATION PANEL
Leah Murray Albert - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Rosetta Bernice Robins - Public Arbitrator
Jon R. Gray - Non-Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Concurring Arbitrators' Signatures
Leah Murray Albert 03/26/2026
